title: The Economist (Web Edition) 30 May 2026 - Part 101

never said that all should have mortgages; just a right to a roof over Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, as “fundamentally sound”, he had had described the two giant government-backed mortgage firms, those who actually blamed him for the sub-prime crash, because he ownership was nice, but not suitable for everyone. As he replied to years he had campaigned for more rental accommodation. Home- Housing, especially for the poor, was a long-term preoccupation. For spare the tender feelings of bankers.

Congress later modified some parts, but he had never meant to (just raising the capital requirements would have been simpler), and forced foreclosures. Some thought it massively over-complicated protection for consumers, eventually returning $21bn to them for that reined in the banks from risky lending and provided more Christopher Dodd, head of the Senate equivalent, he drew up a bill post, head of the House Financial Services Committee. With reform of 2010 that followed the financial crisis. He was in his top The best he did, in his own mind, was the Dodd-Frank banking
